source_section: "12.9"
---

**Proof sketch.** The text states that this is a difficult result, and that it is relatively easy to prove that the substructure generated by $H_1(l), \ldots, H_n(l)$ is a Boolean algebra consisting of $2^n$ elements. The full proof is not reproduced in the source text. To obtain quantum tautologies, one must further consider structures with complements (orthocomplements), substituting the elements of this Boolean algebra for the variables in the usual Boolean tautologies.
